Title: Rice Glycosyltransferase Gene UGT85E1 Is Involved in Drought Stress Tolerance Through Enhancing Abscisic Acid Response

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Biochemical assays testing typical cytokinins (trans-zeatin, cis-zeatin, isopentenyl adenine) and several secondary metabolites (anthocyanins, flavonoids, phenolic acids, phenylpropanoids, auxins, ABA) did not detect significant catalytic activity for UGT85E1 under the conditions used, so its direct substrate(s) remain unidentified. The authors therefore discuss multiple speculative models (e.g., glycosylation of cytokinin(s) or inhibitors of ABA-related transcription factors), but these mechanisms are not experimentally validated in this study.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. UGT85E1 is a rice UDP-dependent glycosyltransferase gene whose expression is strongly induced by mannitol and ABA, indicating involvement in drought-related signaling.
2. Overexpression of UGT85E1 in rice enhances tolerance to mannitol and soil-drought stress, while ugt85e1 knockout mutants are more sensitive; overexpression also confers drought tolerance when ectopically expressed in Arabidopsis.
3. UGT85E1 overexpression increases endogenous ABA levels under drought, promotes stomatal closure, reduces leaf water loss, and heightens ABA sensitivity during seedling growth, concomitant with upregulation of ABA biosynthesis and signaling genes.
4. Overexpression of UGT85E1 enhances ROS-scavenging capacity, reduces H2O2 and superoxide accumulation, increases expression of ROS-scavenging enzyme genes, and leads to higher proline and soluble sugar accumulation, collectively contributing to improved drought and oxidative stress tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression of UGT85E1 enhances drought and oxidative stress tolerance, whereas ugt85e1 knockout mutants are hypersensitive to drought/osmotic and oxidative stresses.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: As a putative UDP-dependent glycosyltransferase, UGT85E1 overexpression leads to increased expression of ABA biosynthesis and signaling genes and ROS-scavenging enzyme genes, resulting in higher ABA levels, enhanced stomatal closure, improved ROS scavenging, and greater accumulation of proline and soluble sugar under drought; the direct biochemical substrate(s) and precise molecular mechanism remain unresolved.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Functions in ABA-dependent drought stress response pathways by elevating ABA accumulation and the expression of ABA biosynthesis (OsABA1, OsABA2, OsABA4, OsNCED1) and ABA-responsive signaling genes (OsABI5, OsbZIP23), and in ROS homeostasis pathways by enhancing ROS-scavenging gene expression and antioxidant capacity.
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: ABA-related metabolic and ROS-scavenging pathways associated with drought and oxidative stress responses (via altered expression of ABA biosynthesis/signaling genes and ROS-scavenging enzyme genes).
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: UGT85E1 -->(activation, at transcript level under drought/mannitol)--> OsABA1, OsABA2, OsABA4, OsNCED1, OsABI5, OsbZIP23 (ABA biosynthesis and signaling genes); UGT85E1 -->(activation)--> OsAPX2, OsCATA, OsCATB, OsFeSODb (ROS-scavenging enzyme genes); UGT85E1 -->(activation)--> OsRAB16, OsRAB21, OsDREB2B, OsDREB1A (drought-responsive genes); UGT85E1 -->(activation, in Arabidopsis overexpression lines)--> AtRD29A, AtRD29B, AtDREB1A, AtDREB1B.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: UDP-dependent glycosyltransferase of the UGT85 family
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: UGT85E1 transcript levels are strongly induced by 200 mM mannitol and 100 μM ABA from 6 to 48 h; expression is upregulated by drought stress.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought (soil water deprivation), osmotic stress via mannitol, ABA treatment, dehydration of detached leaves, and oxidative stress induced by H2O2 and methyl viologen.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Drought stress tolerance, ABA-dependent stress responses, ROS homeostasis, and secondary metabolism regulation in rice.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance (drought tolerance, osmotic stress tolerance, oxidative stress tolerance), stomatal regulation, osmotic adjustment.
Plant_Genes.Favorable_Allele: Overexpressed UGT85E1 allele confers enhanced drought and oxidative stress tolerance compared with wild-type expression or loss-of-function mutants.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: After 2 h dehydration, completely closed stomata comprised 70.0% in OE-3, 30.1% in WT, and 17.3% in ko-8 leaves; overexpression lines showed significantly lower water loss rates over 120 min and higher survival rates than WT under 150 mM mannitol and ~6–10 days of drought, whereas mutants had higher water loss and reduced survival. Overexpression rice and Arabidopsis lines also displayed significantly higher survival after drought recovery and retained higher chlorophyll content under 2% H2O2 or 10 μM MV treatments (all differences P < 0.05).
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: 1) Overexpression lines show greater stomatal closure, reduced water loss from detached leaves, and higher survival rates under mannitol and soil-drought treatments; mutants show more open stomata, greater water loss, and lower survival. 2) Overexpression increases endogenous ABA accumulation and ABA sensitivity (shorter roots under exogenous ABA), while mutants have reduced ABA levels and ABA responsiveness. 3) Overexpression lines accumulate less H2O2 and superoxide under stress, have higher ROS-scavenging enzyme gene expression, and maintain more chlorophyll after H2O2 and MV treatments; mutants show the opposite. 4) Overexpression lines accumulate more proline and soluble sugars under drought, whereas mutants accumulate less. 5) Overexpression upregulates multiple stress-related genes (OsRAB16, OsRAB21, OsDREB2B, OsDREB1A in rice; AtRD29A, AtRD29B, AtDREB1A, AtDREB1B in Arabidopsis).
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Oryza sativa L. subsp. japonica cv. Zhonghua No.11 (wild type); UGT85E1 overexpression rice lines OE-3 and OE-5; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout mutants ko-8 and ko-9; Arabidopsis thaliana ecotype Col-0 overexpression lines OE-4 and OE-7 for ectopic expression assays.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: qRT-PCR to monitor UGT85E1 induction by 200 mM mannitol and 100 μM ABA; generation of overexpression and CRISPR/Cas9 knockout lines; mannitol and soil-drought stress assays with survival rate scoring; measurement of stomatal aperture by scanning electron microscopy; detached-leaf water loss assays; ABA content determination by ELISA; DAB and NBT staining to assess H2O2 and superoxide accumulation; assays of ROS-scavenging enzyme gene expression; quantification of proline, soluble sugar, and chlorophyll contents; H2O2 and methyl viologen treatments for oxidative stress; ABA sensitivity assays during seedling growth.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified from a screen of rice UGT genes responsive to abiotic stress; full-length UGT85E1 cDNA (1,461 bp) cloned into overexpression vectors and CRISPR/Cas9 constructs generated for functional analysis.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: UGT85E1 is a promising candidate gene for improving drought and oxidative stress tolerance in crops. Its overexpression enhances survival and growth under mannitol and soil-drought conditions, increases ABA accumulation and stress-related gene expression, and boosts ROS-scavenging and osmoprotectant accumulation, indicating potential utility in genetic engineering or marker-assisted breeding of drought-tolerant rice and possibly other monocots and dicots.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: This record describes the function of the rice gene UGT85E1 when ectopically expressed in Arabidopsis; the gene remains of Oryza sativa origin and no endogenous Arabidopsis homolog is functionally characterized in this study.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. Rice UGT85E1 driven by the CaMV 35S promoter is successfully overexpressed in Arabidopsis Col-0 lines OE-4 and OE-7.
2. Arabidopsis plants overexpressing UGT85E1 lose water more slowly from detached leaves and show higher survival than WT after prolonged drought and recovery.
3. Ectopic UGT85E1 expression reduces ROS accumulation under mannitol-induced osmotic stress, as shown by weaker DAB and NBT staining.
4. UGT85E1 overexpression elevates transcript levels of abiotic stress-responsive genes AtRD29A, AtRD29B, AtDREB1A, and AtDREB1B in Arabidopsis under mannitol treatment, supporting a conserved role in enhancing drought and oxidative stress tolerance across monocots and dicots.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Ectopic overexpression of rice UGT85E1 in Arabidopsis enhances drought tolerance and reduces ROS accumulation under osmotic stress.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Constitutive expression of rice UGT85E1 in Arabidopsis leads to lower ROS accumulation and higher expression of abiotic stress-responsive transcriptional regulators and marker genes; the direct biochemical mechanism remains unclear.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Rice UGT85E1 acts within Arabidopsis abiotic stress response pathways, reducing ROS accumulation and upregulating canonical stress-responsive genes AtRD29A, AtRD29B, AtDREB1A, and AtDREB1B under mannitol stress.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: UGT85E1 (rice, ectopically expressed in Arabidopsis) -->(activation, at transcript level under mannitol)--> AtRD29A, AtRD29B, AtDREB1A, AtDREB1B; UGT85E1 overexpression -->(inhibition)--> accumulation of ROS detected by DAB and NBT staining.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Transgenic expression of rice UDP-dependent glycosyltransferase UGT85E1 in Arabidopsis background; enzyme belongs to UGT85 family.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Rice UGT85E1 is constitutively overexpressed in Arabidopsis under the CaMV 35S promoter in lines OE-4 and OE-7.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: Rice gene UGT85E1 expressed under CaMV 35S promoter in Arabidopsis; endogenous Arabidopsis reference gene AtActin2 (AT3G18780) used for normalization.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Mannitol-induced osmotic stress (250 mM), soil-drought (6 days without watering) followed by re-watering, and dehydration of detached leaves.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Conservation and heterologous application of a rice glycosyltransferase gene in improving drought and oxidative stress tolerance in Arabidopsis.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance (drought tolerance, osmotic stress), ROS homeostasis.
Plant_Genes.Favorable_Allele: Transgenic overexpression of rice UGT85E1 in Arabidopsis confers a favorable drought-tolerant and ROS-resilient phenotype compared with non-transgenic Col-0.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Under dehydration, OE-4 and OE-7 show significantly lower water loss rates over 120 min and significantly higher survival rates than WT after 6-day drought and 24 h re-watering (P < 0.05).
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Overexpression lines OE-4 and OE-7 exhibit lower water loss rates from detached leaves, higher survival after 6 days drought plus 24 h recovery, greener leaves and better growth than WT, reduced DAB and NBT staining under mannitol treatment, and elevated transcript levels of AtRD29A, AtRD29B, AtDREB1A, and AtDREB1B compared with WT plants.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Arabidopsis thaliana ecotype Col-0 wild type and UGT85E1 overexpression lines OE-4 and OE-7 generated by floral-dip transformation.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: RT-PCR to identify Arabidopsis UGT85E1 overexpression lines; detached-leaf water loss assays; whole-plant drought treatment (6 days without irrigation followed by 24 h re-watering) with survival scoring; DAB and NBT staining after 250 mM mannitol to assess ROS accumulation; qRT-PCR analysis of stress-responsive genes AtRD29A, AtRD29B, AtDREB1A, and AtDREB1B.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Full-length rice UGT85E1 cDNA cloned into pBI121 binary vector under CaMV 35S promoter and transformed into Arabidopsis Col-0 for ectopic functional analysis of drought tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Demonstrates that rice UGT85E1 can enhance drought and oxidative stress tolerance when expressed in a dicot model, supporting its potential use as a transferable transgene to engineer stress-tolerant dicot crops as well as monocots.
